svg.text: Fender Jazz Bass — Parallel / Kill / Series (one DPDT-ON-OFF-ON)
Vol / Vol / Tone | 250kA pots | 0.047µF tone cap | passive
MOD switch (DPDT ON-OFF-ON) — all three operating modes
T3↔T1 closes (neck-GND meets GND rail) + T4↔T2 closes (bridge-HOT meets Vol-B.lug 3)
⇒ PARALLEL: each pickup feeds its own vol pot = standard J-Bass V/V/T
NOTHING closes. Both commons (T3, T4) are floating. Pickups have no complete current path through their coils.
⇒ KILL: both pickups electrically disconnected; BUS ≈ 0 V → silent jack tip
T3↔T5 and T4↔T6 close simultaneously; T5↔T6 jumper ties both commons together
⇒ SERIES: neck-GND ≡ bridge-HOT; coils joined end-to-end, output via Vol-N. Hotter, mid-forward

Pot lugs (viewed from the BACK of the pot): 1 = CCW end, 2 = wiper, 3 = CW end. Volume pots wired hot→lug 3, wiper→out, lug 1→GND, casing (“GND” tab on the pot)→GND. DPDT ON-OFF-ON: terminals 3 & 4 are the pole commons; 1, 2 = throw A (UP); 5, 6 = throw B (DOWN); MIDDLE = both poles open.

Kill action note: with MOD in MIDDLE the jack tip sits at ~0 V but is NOT hard-shorted to GND. A dedicated hard-kill DPDT would actively short tip→GND (cleaner against cable-capacitance pop) — trade-off here is one switch instead of two.
